groups.explanation: The harness correctly writes fuzz bytes to a temp file and calls H5Fopen — valid API usage. The OOM is caused by H5C__load_entry (H5Centry.c:1155) trusting the `actual_len` returned by `get_final_load_size`, which is derived from the file-encoded superblock data. The guard H5C__verify_len_eoa only checks `addr + actual_len <= EOA`, but the EOA value itself is also stored in the superblock (the fuzz input), allowing a crafted file to set both EOA and actual_len to ~3.8 GB while the physical file is tiny. HDF5 must bound speculative allocation sizes against the actual physical file size, not just the file-stated EOA.
groups.suggestedFixDirection: In H5C__verify_len_eoa (or the caller in H5C__load_entry), add a check that `actual_len` does not exceed the actual physical file size (e.g., via H5FD_get_eof or stat on the underlying file descriptor) so that file-encoded size fields cannot force unbounded allocations regardless of the stated EOA.
